title: Smoky Silky Ethiopian Lamb Tacos
description: A unique fusion dish featuring tender lamb, smoky spices, and silky sauce, served in crispy hard taco shells. This kosher-friendly recipe is perfect for a festive dinner.
instructions: Preheat the robata grill to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large bowl, mix together lamb, berbere spice, smoked paprika, salt, and black pepper.
Grill the lamb mixture for 10 minutes, or until cooked through.
Warm the hard taco shells on the grill for 2 minutes.
Assemble the tacos by spooning the lamb mixture into the shells.
Top each taco with silky sauce, chopped onion, and chopped cilantro.
To make the silky sauce, combine 1/2 cup plain yogurt, 1/4 cup chopped fresh parsley, and 1 tablespoon lemon juice in a bowl.
Mix well and refrigerate until ready to use.
Note: silky sauce can be made ahead of time and stored in the refrigerator for up to 24 hours.
